stdout:
[36m[Claude][0m Hold for Claude...


[36m[Claude][0m Hi, I'm Claude — an AI built for meetings. I can surface issues, tickets, and code context on the spot, or capture decisions and action items as the conversation moves.


[36m[Claude][0m Checking the transcript now.


[36m[Claude][0m I don't see a name said to me in the recent transcript or in your chat message — I caught Carol, Bob, Dan, Alice, and Eve speaking, but none of them introduced themselves to me. What's your name?


[36m[Claude][0m Pulling the last 5 minutes of captions.


[36m[Claude][0m Three main threads:


[36m[Claude][0m Launch is blocked on codex parity work. QA found a flaky test in the chat hardening suite, and the auth migration timeline keeps slipping — Sentry is alerting on that path. Rollout proposal is 10% first.


[36m[Claude][0m Key open items: leaked token still needs to be revoked, Priya has a concern on the approval policy, Mohammed pinged on the database pick (Postgres prod / SQLite tests is the working answer), and mobile release branch needs coordination.


[36m[Claude][0m Technical deep-dive from Bob and Dan: marker file beats an env var for startup ordering because the transcript process spawns before the meeting URL exists — they want to confirm it survives a crash. Dan also flagged a risk that the byte ceiling could cause a retry loop if not tuned right.


[36m[Claude][0m Eve noted testing gaps (skipping live Meet tests for DOM observer, auto-leave) but called the risk bounded since those paths haven't been touched this phase.


[36m[Claude][0m One thing, repeated by multiple people across the whole session: Sentry alerts are firing on the auth path. Alice mentioned a dashboard link a few times alongside it. Carol also said "the Sentry alerts are firing again" — suggesting it's a known, ongoing issue, not a one-off. No one resolved it on the call.


[36m[Claude][0m Five speakers: Dan, Carol, Eve, Alice, and Bob. All active — everyone spoke within the last two minutes.


[36m[Claude][0m Same themes as the rest of the meeting — the marker file vs. env var debate (Eve and Carol both explained the startup ordering argument), Sentry alerts on the auth path, the database pick, leaked token still unrevoked, and a flaky QA test. No distinct new topic in that window; it was continuity of the same discussion.


[36m[Claude][0m Yes — repeatedly, across the whole session. The consistent message from Carol, Dan, Bob, Eve, and Alice: launch is blocked on codex parity work. Alice also specifically said "I want to bring up the launch timeline" early on (16:45). No one indicated it was unblocked.


[36m[Claude][0m Based on everything I've pulled this session:


[36m[Claude][0m Headline: launch is blocked on codex parity work. That came up ~90 times from all five speakers — it's the through-line of the whole meeting.


[36m[Claude][0m Key blockers and open items:
- Sentry alerts firing on the auth path all session, never resolved. Alice referenced a dashboard link multiple times.
- Auth migration timeline keeps slipping.
- Leaked token still needs to be revoked.
- QA found a flaky test in the chat hardening suite — suggestion to write a fixture for the edge case.
- Priya raised a concern about the approval policy (Priya wasn't on the call — mentioned by others).
- Mohammed pinged about the database pick (also not on the call). Working answer: Postgres for prod, SQLite for tests.
- Need to coordinate with the mobile release branch.


[36m[Claude][0m Technical decisions landed:
- Marker file beats env var for startup ordering — codex spawns the transcript process before the meeting URL exists, so the path can't be baked into spawn args. Dan wants to confirm it survives a crash.
- Dan flagged a byte ceiling risk: if tuned wrong, the model could retry on truncation indefinitely (thrashing loop).
- Eve acknowledged testing gaps — skipping live Meet tests for DOM observer, auto-leave, meet.new resolution. Called the risk bounded since those paths weren't touched this phase.


[36m[Claude][0m Positive signals: wizard re-import flow is finally idempotent, prompt cache hit rate looked great, new Sonnet benchmarks strong on coding tasks.


[36m[Claude][0m Proposed: narrow rollout to 10% first.


[36m[Claude][0m The only substantive quotes are these two, repeated throughout by multiple people:


[36m[Claude][0m "Mohammed pinged about the database pick." — Bob (16:44), echoed by Alice, Carol, Dan, Eve across the session.


[36m[Claude][0m "Postgres for prod SQLite for tests is the standard split." — Carol (16:45), Dan (17:43).


[36m[Claude][0m No one debated it further on the call — it reads like Carol and Dan named the answer and the room moved on.

         (subscription)

queries: what name did I just say
what did anyone say about Sentry
what was the topic 8 to 10 minutes ago
quote the part where someone talked about the database
